question: Read: 'Wearables and other smart devices, such as clothing, could be used to track your body's fat and water content.' What are 'wearables'?
choices: Electronic devices that can be worn on the body

question: Read: 'Some people are concerned that it is easy to hack into a self-driving car, or that there might be a malfunction causing an accident.' What is a 'malfunction'?
choices: A fault that prevents something from working correctly

question: Read: 'Your front door will use facial recognition software to decide if you should be allowed into the house or not.' This sentence uses which passive form?
In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', what problem might you face when you buy a new device as an early adopter?
choices: The device runs out of battery quickly
The device is too heavy to carry
Compatibility issues requiring new adaptors or cables
The device cannot connect to Wi-Fi

question: Which word describes software that is 'not working very well because it's rather old'?
Which word correctly completes: 'That style of trousers looks really ________ now.'?
The phrasal verb 'back up' means ________.
choices: to return to a previous location
to store a copy of information on a device so it won't be lost
to support someone in an argument

question: Read from the VR article: 'The doors shut with a thud behind me as I walk into the shopping centre. Immediately, there's a buzz of noise. I can hear two or three different types of music being played in various shops.' This first paragraph is written to ________.
choices: show how overwhelming everyday situations can be for someone with ASD
explain how VR works in shopping environments

question: Read from the VR article: 'Some people are able to cope with this kind of sensory overload reasonably well, though the stress might eat away at them inside.' What does 'eat away at' mean in this context?
choices: To gradually harm or affect someone internally
To break off from an activity

question: Read from the VR article: 'It can also lead to people with autism having problems at school or with holding down a job, as they may come across as unreliable or bad-tempered.' 'Come across as' means ________.
choices: to appear to others in a certain way

question: Read from the VR article: 'Those people who had been chopping down trees reached for fewer paper napkins, suggesting that their experience had subconsciously encouraged them to stop wasting paper.' The word 'subconsciously' means ________.
choices: in a way that is deliberate and planned
in a way that happens without the person being aware of it
in a way that is completely obvious
in a way that is physically felt

question: Read: 'A major difference between simulated and actual experiences is that we volunteer to put on the VR headset. We can break off at any time.' What does this tell us about VR?
choices: VR is more comfortable than real experience
Unlike real sufferers, VR users can choose to stop the experience at any time
VR experiences last much longer than real experiences
VR users feel less empathy than people who have real experiences

question: In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'Relax — it's going to be OK' should be replaced by ________.
In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'I didn't expect to see you here!' should be replaced by ________.
Read from the VR article: 'VR has also been used to give people the experience of being disabled, or being in a flood.' What is the PURPOSE of these experiences?
choices: To entertain people with exciting virtual experiences
To help people understand the challenges others face in their real lives
To test the technical limits of VR equipment
To replace real emergency training programmes

question: The word 'deceptive' means ________.
choices: misleading or giving a false impression

question: Read: 'People started taking selfies not just to present a flattering picture of themselves, but also to manage and curate what they wanted to tell the world about their physical attributes, their personality, their relationships and their hobbies.' The word 'curate' here means to ________.
choices: carefully select and organise what you share about yourself
filter photos using a smartphone app

question: Read: 'On a superficial level, their holiday photos look relaxed and natural, but in reality they've been very carefully planned — and paid for.' What does this passage reveal?
choices: That holiday photos are always natural and genuine
That social media appearances can be carefully constructed and misleading
That planning photos is always a good idea
That photographers always produce natural-looking photos

question: Read: 'You can add filters to enhance how you look, remove any flaws or even change the shape or size of your facial features.' The word 'flaws' means ________.
Read: 'In my considered opinion, online learning should be combined with traditional methods.' The phrase 'in my considered opinion' is used in the ________.

Read from the Bell article: 'In March 1876, the Scottish scientist Alexander Graham Bell called his assistant Thomas Watson and said, Mr Watson, come here, I want to see you. These are thought to have been the first words ever spoken on a telephone.' Which impersonal passive pattern is used?
choices: Pattern a: it + passive + that clause
Pattern c: subject + passive + perfect infinitive
Active voice with a reporting verb

question: Rewrite using Pattern (b): 'Initially, people thought the telephone was only for rich people.' → Initially, the telephone ________ to have been only for rich people.
Rewrite using Pattern (a): 'Experts expect that more people will own phones in the future.' → It ________ that more people will own phones in the future.
In the essay writing guide, which phrase is used to introduce arguments AGAINST the topic?
choices: There may be some clear benefits to doing …
In spite of these potential benefits, many people …
In conclusion, it seems obvious that …

question: Read: 'Although most people are careful with data such as online banking details, many actually prefer more personalised marketing.' The word 'although' is a ________.
According to the Life Skills section on social media, social media allows us to belong to groups of people with the same ________.
According to the Life Skills section, when applying to university or for a job, people might look at your social media ________.
In the Life Skills matching exercise, Tip 4 'Do a search on your name every six months or so' is matched with ________.
choices: Remember that posting privately doesn't protect you
Ask yourself if your grandmother would be comfortable reading it
Set up an alert to let you know when someone mentions you online
Don't share anything belonging to someone else without permission

question: Choose the correct passive: 'This device shouldn't ________ by anyone under the age of 16.'
Choose the correct passive: 'The teacher congratulated them on ________ to take part in the science fair.'
In the passive exercises, 'The first real smartphone ________ by a famous company.' (create)
In the passive exercises, 'This app has ________ by over two million people.' (download)
In the passive exercises, 'Celebrities' smartphones ________ regularly hacked into.' (past continuous passive)
Read: 'Many teens work on their online image. Some of them ________ their image for fun, others feel it is an important part of who they are.' Which word fits?
Read: 'Teens use their smart devices to blog, comment, and ________ their ideas.' Which word fits?
Read from the Bell article: 'Not everybody believes Alexander Graham Bell was the inventor of the telephone.' Which of the following is correct according to the article?
choices: Everyone agrees Bell invented the telephone
The Italians claim that Antonio Meucci is the true inventor
The phone was invented by a team of scientists
Bell refused to take credit for the invention

question: Read from the Bell article: 'It is estimated that there are more than 8.5 billion mobile phones in a world that has a population of around 8 billion people.' This means ________.
choices: Fewer phones than people exist in the world
The number of phones equals the number of people
There are more phones than people in the world
The number of phones is slightly fewer than people

question: Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'In conclusion, it seems obvious that while people remain largely unaware and unable to make informed choices, the ability of companies to collect data should be limited or they should be required to specifically ask for permission.' What is the main recommendation?
choices: Companies should be encouraged to collect more data
People should be taught more about data privacy
Companies should be limited in collecting data or required to ask permission first

question: Read: 'Basically, you should behave online as you would in real life. Don't make rude comments, don't steal other people's property.' Which Life Skills tip does this represent?
choices: Don't post anything without thinking about consequences
Take steps to hide anything negative
The same rules apply online as in real life

question: Read: 'If you follow someone on social media, make sure they are influencing you in a positive way. If you think this might not be true, you can opt out of content that does not positively affect you.' 'Opt out' means to ________.
choices: choose to stop receiving or participating in something
report someone to the social media platform
